blurb: Vitalik Buterin said Ethereum's multi-year "Lean Ethereum" rebuild will replace nearly every major piece of the protocol over three to four years, calling it the network's third major iteration on par with the Merge. He flagged a redesign of on-chain data storage as the most disruptive change, one that could cut some token fees more than tenfold without forcing legacy apps to rewrite their code.

title: NSE Said to Start Marketing $3 Billion India IPO Next Week
blurb: The National Stock Exchange of India, operator of the world's largest derivatives exchange by trading volume, will begin formal marketing of its roughly $3B IPO as early as next week, according to people familiar with the matter. The listing would rank among India's largest ever and gives global investors direct exposure to the exchange infrastructure underpinning the country's booming equity-derivatives market.

title: Korean Won’s 24-Hour Trading Era Begins With Cheers, Applause
blurb: The South Korean won began 24-hour trading on July 5, extending its onshore currency market to a full-day schedule as part of Seoul's multi-year push to modernize its FX infrastructure and win developed-market status. The move deepens access for foreign institutional investors and settlement desks that previously worked around Korea's limited trading window, aligning won liquidity closer to global standards.

title: Bitcoin ETFs log record eighth straight negative week despite large Thursday inflow
blurb: U.S. spot bitcoin ETFs shed about $527 million over the four-session holiday week, their eighth consecutive negative week and the longest outflow run since the funds launched. BlackRock's IBIT extended its own redemption streak to 11 days despite Thursday's $221.72 million single-day inflow, leaving the flagship fund at $44.91 billion in assets against $59.99 billion in cumulative inflows and the average investor down roughly 40%.

title: Banks have stopped asking if stablecoins belong in finance, now they're considering how
blurb: Standard Chartered began offering institutional clients direct minting and redemption of Circle's USDC this week, days after BNY, the world's largest custody bank at $59T in assets, expanded USDC custody and issuance support. Both moves signal that global systemically important banks are integrating existing stablecoin networks rather than launching their own, with Chainalysis projecting stablecoin settlement volumes could reach a quadrillion dollars annually by 2030.

title: Kalshi and prediction market sector embroiled in mixed bag of legal fights across U.S.
blurb: Kalshi and the broader prediction-market industry are fighting simultaneous legal battles across multiple states over whether their event contracts are regulated derivatives or illegal gambling, losing recent bids in Nevada and Michigan while pressing arguments in Minnesota and Ohio. The CFTC's backing gives the industry a federal ally, but definitive court rulings remain unsettled, and North Carolina is moving to tax prediction-market revenue as the sector's legal footing stays in question.

title: Collateral, not yield, will decide which stablecoins win
blurb: In an opinion piece, Falcon Finance's Artem Tolkachev argues that stablecoin issuers are optimizing for the wrong metric as yield-bearing tokens race toward a $50B market cap. Yield is trivially copied and competed away, so holders rotate to whatever pays a few basis points more. What actually drives usage is collateral acceptance: whether exchanges and lending markets take the token as margin at sensible loan-to-value.

title: Clarity and Congress's summer break: State of Crypto
blurb: The Clarity Act missed its hoped-for July 4 signing, and with Congress on summer recess and the midterms approaching, backers now have a narrow window to move the crypto market-structure bill through both chambers this year. Staffers are still reconciling the Senate Agriculture and Banking Committee versions behind the scenes, but a House or Senate flip in November would likely reopen negotiations and delay any framework for classifying digital assets well into 2027.

title: China’s Biggest ETF Is Now a Gold Fund as National Team Retreats
blurb: A China spot gold ETF has overtaken the Huatai-PineBridge CSI 300 ETF to become the country's largest exchange-traded fund, a shift that signals state-backed "national team" buying has retreated from equities. The rotation into gold underscores waning official support for the stock market and a broader flight to safe-haven assets as Chinese investors hedge against a weak economy and currency.

title: Thought Machine lands $40M funding from bank, surpasses $100M in annual revenue
blurb: Thought Machine raised £30M ($41M) from an unnamed Tier 1 bank client, bringing its enlarged round to £80M as annual revenue topped $100M in 2025, a 57% year-over-year jump. CEO Paul Taylor downplayed valuation, framing revenue growth as the better success metric and putting a London IPO at least two years out amid difficult market conditions.
